content.problemStatement: Write a program to output the squares (using multiplication) of numbers from 1 to 5 on separate lines.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. No further input.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the squares of numbers from 1 to 5, each on its own line in the format "i - i*i". The outputs of consecutive test cases are printed one after another without extra blank lines.

sampleTestCases.explanation: With a single test case, the program prints the squares of 1 through 5 once.
Two test cases cause the block of five lines to be printed twice consecutively.
Three test cases repeat the pattern three times.

solution.approach: The problem is deterministic: for every test case we have to output the same five lines. Read T, then repeat a fixed block of five lines T times. The algorithm runs in O(T) time and uses O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) overall, O(1) per test case.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    T = int(data[0])
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        out_lines.append('1 - 1')
        out_lines.append('2 - 4')
        out_lines.append('3 - 9')
        out_lines.append('4 - 16')
        out_lines.append('5 - 25')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines) + ('\n' if out_lines else ''))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
